champion_kor_blackstar:idle.greeting: Kor Blackstar lowers his mask just enough to talk normally — not far enough to show his whole face. "Walker. The city's quiet tonight. That usually means it isn't."

nodes.npcText: "You're here on the roof, which means you're a walker who isn't afraid of heights. That narrows it down."

playerOptions.text: I've come about your old Sifu — he's alive in the hub.
I want to talk to you about Iron Hex.

nodes.npcText: The mask comes back up an inch. "That's not possible. He died when I was nineteen."

playerOptions.text: I've met him. He tends roses.

nodes.npcText: A long pause. "If you're telling the truth, you have changed my year. If you're lying, I will find out." He nods once. "Tell me where."

playerOptions.text: Taro Sandren's brawling pit, hub. Mornings.

nodes.npcText: His voice does not change. "Iron Hex was my apprentice. I failed him. If you came to tell me to stop saying that, save your breath."

playerOptions.text: I came to ask if you'd meet him.
I came to ask why you haven't killed him.

nodes.npcText: "On neutral ground. No mask. No suit. If you can arrange that, do. Bring him to the rooftop above the noodle shop on 7th. Midnight, any Wednesday."
"Because I owe him better than killing him. Don't ask me that again."
He turns toward the edge. The cape catches. He's gone before you can answer.

reporter_mira_vance:idle.greeting: Mira Vance is on her third coffee of the morning. "Walker. Give me thirty seconds — I have a deadline."

nodes.npcText: "Twenty-eight seconds. What."

playerOptions.text: You're building a profile on Champion's identity. Don't publish.
Quick question about a story you ran.

nodes.npcText: The coffee comes down hard. "How do you know that?"

playerOptions.text: I know. Don't ask. Burn the profile.

nodes.npcText: "I won't burn it. I can sit on it. Tell Champion that. I'm a journalist, not a vigilante."
She studies you. "That would be more impressive if it weren't also a thing a liar would say. I'll sit on the story for now. Don't waste my discretion."
"Speak."

playerOptions.text: Officer Carver is negotiating a truce with Iron Hex.

nodes.npcText: "That's a column. That's two columns. If I run this, the city erupts before the truce takes hold. Tell me who told you so I can verify."
"Which one?"
She is already typing again. "Get out of my doorway."

mentor_old_silas:idle.greeting: Silas Crane straightens from his rose bed, garden gloves caked with soil. "Walker. The roses are blooming early this year. Sit if you like; the bench is dry."

nodes.npcText: "I haven't worn the mask in twenty years. Don't ask me to. Anything else, I'll answer."

playerOptions.text: Tell me about Iron Hex. The truth.

nodes.npcText: He sits on the bench. "His name is Avery. He was my best student. He believed harder than I did. I broke first — quit, retired, came home to roses. He didn't. Now he wears that armour."
"Kor figures it out without help, or he doesn't. I won't tell him. If you do, you'll be the agent of that, not me."
"Because I started winning fights I shouldn't have. That kind of certainty kills more people than uncertainty. The roses are honest. They die when you stop watering them."
He returns to the rose bed. "Watch your step on the path."
